title: Magma pebble

'''Magma pebbles''' were non-organic thrown weapons utilized by the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]] during their invasion of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Consisting of a shell filled with a thumbnail-sized glob of [[Magma/Legends|magma]], the pebbles were primarily used to counter the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] that the Yuuzhan Vong [[Warrior caste|warriors]] encountered while fighting, as the magma contained within could heavily damage the circuits and [[Armor/Legends|armor]] of [[Droid/Legends|droids]].

==Description==
[[Magma/Legends|Magma]] pebbles, unlike the majority of weapons of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]], were inorganic in nature<ref name="Fact" /> and consisted of a hard shell containing a thumbnail-sized glob<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|II|275|magma pebble}}</ref> of magma. Small enough to fit in the palm of a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid's]] [[Limb/Legends|hand]],<ref name="Fact" /> magma pebbles heated up due to any agitation, such as throwing them, which would cause them to shed their shell<ref name="TCSWE" /> and unleash their payload of molten rock. The magma contained within the [[Color/Legends|white]] and orangey–red pebble was potent enough to eat through the outer casing of a [[YVH-Series battle droid]] and destroy the inner circuits within [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]].<ref name="Fact" />

Magma pebbles were the most utilized non-organic weapon for the [[Warrior caste|warriors]] of the Yuuzhan Vong and were mainly used as thrown weapons against [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. The magma contained within the pebble was effective at eating into the [[Armor/Legends|armor]] of droids and disabling and destroying them. Magma pebbles were hurled at the opponent, with the shell dissolving in midair or breaking open on impact to release the magma. In addition to their effectiveness against droids, magma pebbles could also be used on organic lifeforms, as the molten payload could cause extensive and severe burns.<ref name="Fact" />

==History==
In use by [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' to [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]].</ref> during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], when the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] Yuuzhan Vong invaded [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], magma pebbles were used by the warrior caste against the droids that were deployed against them in battle. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] crew of the Yuuzhan Vong [[I'Friil Ma-Nat|frigate]] ''[[Exquisite Death]]'' employed magma pebbles and organic [[Nang hul|thud bugs]] in a fruitless attempt to stop the YVH-Series [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[YVH 2-1S|2-1S]] during a [[Mission to Myrkr|seizure]] of the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] by [[Myrkr strike team|members]] of the [[New Jedi Order]], though the assault left the droid's [[Laminanium/Legends|laminanium]] armor pitted.<ref name="Star" /> Magma pebbles nevertheless proved very effective during the war in damaging YVH droids,<ref name="Fact" /> which were designed specifically to combat Yuuzhan Vong warriors.<ref name="Star" />

==Behind the scenes==
Magma pebbles first appeared in [[Troy Denning]]'s novel [[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star|''Star by Star'']], the [[2001]] ninth installment of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series.<ref name="Star" /> The subject received a visual depiction in [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 111|issue 111]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'', released in [[2004]].<ref name="Fact" /> The weapon also received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]]. However, the encyclopedia entry states that magma pebbles were filled with [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]], rather than magma, as stated in the Fact File.<ref name="TCSWE" /> This article treats the Fact File information as being correct, considering it goes into more detail regarding magma pebbles.
